evals.prompt: I have results showing that a novel anti-PD-L1 antibody combined with a STING agonist significantly improves tumor regression in a syngeneic mouse model compared to either agent alone. The combination achieved complete regression in 60% of mice vs 15% for anti-PD-L1 alone. Help me draft the Introduction section for a Nature Medicine submission.
evals.expected_output: A well-structured Introduction following Glasman-Deal's 4-component model (establish importance → literature review → gap → present work), with appropriate tense usage, gap language, and Nature Medicine style (concise, unstructured format)

evals.prompt: We ran a Phase 2, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial of empagliflozin 10mg vs placebo in 240 patients with HFpEF (LVEF >= 50%). Primary endpoint was change in NT-proBNP at 12 weeks. Randomization was 1:1 stratified by diabetes status. IRB approved by Mass General Brigham (protocol #2024-001234). Write the Methods section.
evals.expected_output: A complete Methods section with ethical approvals, study design, participant eligibility, intervention details, outcomes, and statistical analysis plan, following CONSORT structure

evals.prompt: I'm writing a review on CAR-T cell therapy for solid tumors. I need 3-5 verified citations supporting the claim that the immunosuppressive tumor microenvironment is a major barrier to CAR-T efficacy in solid tumors. Search PubMed and give me verified BibTeX entries.
evals.expected_output: 3-5 real, PubMed-verified citations with BibTeX entries, each with PMID and DOI confirmed, supporting the specific claim about tumor microenvironment barriers to CAR-T therapy
